Cytopathology & Flow Cytometry Report – Lymph Node
FNA
Department: Laboratory Medicine – Cytopathology
Accession: NSHNICYTOI21I10477
Collection Date: 2021-10-25
Ordering Clinician: ENT / Head & Neck Surgery
Specimen Site: Left neck, level II lymph node
Fixation/Handling: AirIdried and alcoholIfixed smears; cell block prepared
**Specimen:** Fine needle aspirate, left cervical lymph node (zone II), ultrasound-guided. Two direct smears and
a cell block were prepared; a separate tube was submitted for flow cytometry.
**Clinical History (provided):** Persistent cervical lymphadenopathy with ultrasound showing a dominant level II
node with cortical thickening. Request: exclude lymphoma or metastatic malignancy.
**Cytology (Microscopy):** Smears show a polymorphous lymphoid population with small mature lymphocytes,
scattered centroblast-like forms, and occasional immunoblasts. Tingible-body macrophages are present,
supporting reactive follicular activity. No granulomas are identified. No necrosis is seen. No cohesive epithelial
clusters are present to suggest metastatic carcinoma.
**Cell Block:** Limited cellularity; fragments of lymphoid tissue show reactive-appearing lymphocytes without
overt atypia. Architecture assessment is limited by the nature of FNA material.
**Ancillary Stains:** Papanicolaou stain negative for malignant epithelial cells. No organisms identified on routine
review (special stains not requested on this specimen).', '6 L

response: The FNA pathology report concluded that the smears showed a polymorphous lymphoid population with small mature lymphocytes, scattered centroblast-like forms, and occasional immunoblasts, indicating reactive follicular activity. There were no granulomas, necrosis, or cohesive epithelial clusters suggestive of metastatic carcinoma. The cell block showed reactive-appearing lymphocytes without overt atypia, though architecture assessment was limited. No malignant epithelial cells or organisms were identified. Overall, the findings did not support lymphoma or metastatic malignancy.

Endoscopy Report – EGD and Colonoscopy with Biopsies
Facility: Thornhill Endoscopy Centre (Affiliated with NorthStar Health Network)
Procedures: EGD + Colonoscopy (CSP)
Date of Procedure: 2022-01-20
Endoscopist: Gastroenterology (network affiliate)
Specimens: Gastric antrum/body biopsies; terminal ileum biopsies
Disposition: Home same day with standard post-procedure instructions
**Indication:** Evaluation of gastrointestinal tract given ongoing systemic workup for chronic lymphadenopathy
and concern for inflammatory or malignant pathology. Family history includes ulcerative colitis (per longitudinal
notes).
**Procedures:** Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) and colonoscopy with terminal ileum intubation.
Conscious sedation administered. Continuous monitoring. No immediate complications.
**EGD Findings:** Esophagus normal without erosions, rings, or varices. Stomach: mild antral erythema
compatible with **mild chronic gastritis**; biopsies obtained from antrum and body. No ulcers or mass lesions.
Duodenum normal to second portion; biopsies not obtained from duodenum.
**Colonoscopy Findings:** Cecum reached; withdrawal performed with careful mucosal inspection. Colonic
mucosa grossly normal without polyps or masses. Terminal ileum intubated ~10 cm; mucosa demonstrates
subtle erythema and granular change without deep ulceration. Biopsies obtained from terminal ileum.', 'Colonic
mucosa grossly normal without polyps or masses. Terminal ileum intubated ~10 cm; mucosa demonstrates
subtle erythema and granular change without deep ulceration. Biopsies obtained from terminal ileum.
**Pathology:** (1) Gastric antrum: mild chronic gastritis; **no Helicobacter pylori** identified. (2) Terminal ileum:
**mild chronic active ileitis**. (3) No dysplasia or malignancy identified in submitted specimens.
**Assessment/Comment:** Mild ileitis can be nonspecific and may relate to infection, NSAID exposure, or early
inflammatory bowel disease; clinical correlation required. In the absence of diarrhea, abdominal pain, or weight
loss, immediate escalation may not be required.
**Recommendations:** Follow with primary/internal medicine for symptom correlation and biomarkers. If new GI
symptoms arise, consider fecal calprotectin, repeat inflammatory markers, and gastroenterology followIup.', '**Results Reviewed – Urine Studies (2025I03I24):** Urinalysis within normal limits; urine culture negative; urine
cytology negative; albuminIcreatinine ratio within normal limits. CRP 9.1 mg/L (elevated).
**Results Reviewed – Ultrasound (2025I03I24):** MultiIsite ultrasound of neck, bilateral groin,
abdomen/pelvis, and bilateral axilla demonstrates wellIdefined hypoechoic masses in the anterior neck with
**thick cortical mantles**. Dominant right level 2A node measures **37.8 × 11.5 × 27.5 mm** (larger than prior).
Additional right level 2A node **23.3 × 5.8 × 19.9 mm** (new). Left level 2A node **22.3 × 9.4 × 19.6 mm**
(larger) and left level 2B node **19.9 × 7.8 × 16.2 mm** (larger). Groin: wellIdefined nodes medial to
neurovascular bundle up to **24 × 7.5 × 13.8 mm** on the right and **15.3 × 4.2 × 12.8 mm** on the left,
described with thin cortical mantles. Axillae: subcutaneous nodes with thin cortical mantles; dominant right
axillary nodes **21.5 × 7.7 × 10.8 mm** and **18.8 × 5.8 × 7.9 mm**; dominant left axillary nodes **25.5 × 8.4 ×
10.4 mm** and **16.4 × 5.6 × 8.3 mm**. Renal ultrasound normal bilaterally; prostate volume 13.2 cc; postIvoid
residual 63 cc.
**Assessment:** Persistent, enlarging, asymptomatic multiIstation lymphadenopathy of unclear etiology with
intermittently rising CRP. Recurrent microscopic hematuria under surveillance with reassuring workup. Incidental
ileitis on prior colonoscopy without GI symptoms.']
response: The GI endoscopy revealed a normal esophagus without erosions, rings, or varices. The stomach showed mild antral erythema consistent with mild chronic gastritis, with biopsies taken from the antrum and body. The duodenum appeared normal up to the second portion, with no biopsies obtained. During colonoscopy, the cecum was reached and the colonic mucosa appeared grossly normal without polyps or masses. The terminal ileum was intubated about 10 cm and showed subtle erythema and granular changes without deep ulceration, with biopsies taken from this area. Pathology showed mild chronic gastritis in the gastric antrum without Helicobacter pylori, mild chronic active ileitis in the terminal ileum, and no dysplasia or malignancy in the specimens.
reference: GI endoscopy results from 2022.

granulomas). Overall malignancy risk low given size and pattern.', 'NorthStar Health Network

Ultrasound Report – Neck Soft Tissues
Department: Diagnostic Imaging – Ultrasound
Facility: NorthStar Imaging Centre – Toronto
Exam: Ultrasound Neck (Bilateral)
Date of Exam: 2021-10-18
Comparison: None available at time of interpretation
**Indication:** Palpable left upper anterior neck mass / lymph node. Persistent LAN without constitutional
symptoms. Evaluate morphology and guide decision for sampling.
**Technique:** HighIfrequency linear transducer ultrasound of bilateral neck soft tissues with targeted
assessment of level II and adjacent nodal stations. Color Doppler used to assess vascularity. Measurements
recorded in three dimensions where feasible.
**Findings:** In the left level II region (upper anterior cervical chain), a dominant lymph node is identified with
**moderately thickened cortical mantle** and preserved overall oval configuration. The hilum is partially
visualized. The dominant node measures approximately **32 × 10 × 28 mm**. A second, smaller node in the
same chain demonstrates mild cortical prominence but maintains a benign-appearing shape. Right-sided level II
nodes are present and mildly prominent; cortical thickness is less pronounced than the dominant left node. No
discrete thyroid mass is seen on the limited field of view. There is no drainable fluid collection.
**Doppler:** Hilar vascularity is present; there is no marked peripheral hypervascularity.
**Impression:** (1) Dominant left level II lymph node with cortical thickening; morphology is **atypical** for simple
reactive adenopathy but not definitively malignant.', 'Heart size normal. No pericardial effusion. Great vessels unremarkable within
non-angiographic limits.
**Findings – Upper Abdomen (limited):** Visualized liver, spleen, adrenals, and upper kidneys demonstrate no
acute abnormality within the limited field.
**Impression:** (1) Bilateral subcentimeter pulmonary nodules up to 4 mm; appearance favors benign etiology in
low-risk patient. (2) No intrathoracic lymphadenopathy. (3) Mild emphysematous change—correlate with
exposure history and consider spirometry if clinically indicated.
**Recommendation:** Optional surveillance CT in 6–12 months depending on risk factors and shared
decision-making; if prior imaging exists, comparison for stability is valuable.']
response: The chest CT showed multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules measuring up to 4 mm without spiculation or associated lymphadenopathy. There was mild emphysematous background change noted. No intrathoracic lymphadenopathy was present. The nodules appeared consistent with benign etiology in a low-risk patient.
reference: Chest CT findings from 2022.

**Specimen:** Fine needle aspirate, left cervical lymph node (zone II), ultrasound-guided. Two direct smears and
a cell block were prepared; a separate tube was submitted for flow cytometry.

node with cortical thickening. Request: exclude lymphoma or metastatic malignancy.

review (special stains not requested on this specimen).']
